 {"id":"gt-rixa","title":"Bug: parseLifecycleRequest always matches 'cycle' due to LIFECYCLE prefix","description":"In lifecycle.go, parseLifecycleRequest checks strings.Contains(title, "cycle") first, but the prefix "LIFECYCLE:" contains the word "cycle". This means ALL lifecycle messages match the cycle action, making restart and shutdown unreachable. Fix: check for restart/shutdown before cycle, or use word boundaries.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:16.083512-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.83084-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rm3","title":"CLI: gt refinery commands (start, stop, status, queue)","description":"CLI commands for managing the Refinery agent.

## Commands

```bash
gt refinery start <rig>      # Start refinery for a rig
gt refinery stop <rig>       # Stop refinery
gt refinery status <rig>     # Show refinery status
gt refinery queue <rig>      # Show merge queue
```

## gt refinery start

Starts the Refinery daemon for the specified rig.

Options:
- --foreground: Run in foreground (default: background)
- --auto-merge: Enable auto-merge (default: from config)

## gt refinery stop

Stops a running Refinery. Gracefully finishes current MR if processing.

## gt refinery status

Shows:
- Running state (running/stopped)
- Current MR being processed (if any)
- Queue length
- Last merge time
- Recent activity

## gt refinery queue

Shows the merge queue:
```
Merge queue for 'wyvern':
  1. [pending] Toast/polecat-auth-fix (15m ago)
  2. [pending] Capable/polecat-new-feature (5m ago)
  
1 merged today, 0 rejected
```

## Implementation

Uses gt-ov2 (Refinery agent) for daemon functionality.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:24.754361-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T14:16:34.593637-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rm3","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:22:30.679909-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-rp0k","title":"Extend auto-continue to polecats (not just crew)","description":"gt prime currently only outputs AUTO-CONTINUE MODE for crew workers.
Polecats with attached work should also auto-continue.

## Current behavior
outputCrewAttachmentStatus() in prime.go:
- Only runs for RoleCrew
- Outputs '→ AUTO-CONTINUE MODE' when attached work detected

## Desired behavior
- Rename to outputAttachmentStatus() or similar
- Run for RoleCrew AND RolePolecat
- Same directive: if attachment exists, work immediately

## The Propulsion Principle
'If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.'

This applies to ALL workers, not just crew.

## Implementation
1. Extend role check in outputCrewAttachmentStatus()
2. Adjust assignee detection for polecats vs crew
3. Test with both worker types","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-rp0k","title":"Extend auto-continue to polecats (not just crew)","description":"gt prime currently only outputs AUTO-CONTINUE MODE for crew workers.
Polecats with attached work should also auto-continue.

## Current behavior
outputCrewAttachmentStatus() in prime.go:
- Only runs for RoleCrew
- Outputs '→ AUTO-CONTINUE MODE' when attached work detected

## Desired behavior
- Rename to outputAttachmentStatus() or similar
- Run for RoleCrew AND RolePolecat
- Same directive: if attachment exists, work immediately

## The Propulsion Principle
'If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.'

This applies to ALL workers, not just crew.

## Implementation
1. Extend role check in outputCrewAttachmentStatus()
2. Adjust assignee detection for polecats vs crew
3. Test with both worker types","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:22.149252-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:37:54.539187-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rr1i","title":"mol-swarm-cleanup: Post-swarm debris cleanup molecule","description":"After a 20+ worker swarm completed, found significant beads debris:
- 18 stale messages (work assignments, lifecycle requests, swarm instructions)
- 3 completed issues still open/in_progress
- Test messages accumulated

Need: Document a post-swarm checklist or create gt swarm cleanup command that:
1. Closes stale work assignment messages
2. Reviews in_progress issues for completion
3. Closes orphaned lifecycle messages
4. Optionally archives test messages","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:12:28.646175-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:15:45.521085-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rt6g","title":"Bug: Deacon session linked to Mayor pane causes heartbeat crosstalk","description":"**Root cause found**: The gt-deacon tmux session was linked to gt-mayor window 2 - they shared the same pane (@283). When the daemon sent heartbeats to the Deacon, they appeared as typed input in the Mayor's window, interrupting user prompts.

**Fix applied**: Killed the broken gt-deacon session. The daemon auto-recreates it with a proper independent pane.

**Prevention**: Investigate how sessions can get linked and add safeguards to session creation code.

Original symptom: Mayor receiving 'HEARTBEAT: Tip: Witnesses monitor polecats...' messages that ate user input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:00:28.667896-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:08:19.974928-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:08:19.974928-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: killed linked gt-deacon session. Daemon will recreate with independent pane."}
 {"id":"gt-ruw","title":"Fix TestHasPolecat test failure in internal/session","description":"TestHasPolecat in internal/session/manager_test.go fails because it expects
specific polecats (Toast, Cheedo) to exist in the test environment.

Error:
```
manager_test.go:46: expected hasPolecat(Toast) = true
manager_test.go:49: expected hasPolecat(Cheedo) = true
```

Fix: Either create test fixtures or mock the filesystem check.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:02:30.030032-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T11:58:56.1846-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T11:58:56.1846-08:00"}
